ActionItems: Convert three pending compliance retainers to signed monthly agreements by Q4.
Add two new manufacturing clients to lift recurring retainer revenue above $20k/mo.
Hold software and bond expenses flat while audit project volume grows.
BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Monthly break-even review for Kim Trade Services, a 6-person customs brokerage and trade compliance firm in Columbus, OH. Revenue is project fees plus monthly retainers; no subscriptions.

CurrentState.Note: Annualized gross ~$700k. Owner distributions ~$2,000/mo are taken separately from the net draw.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Recurring retainer revenue covers about 41 percent of monthly expenses. Each additional $2,500 retainer moves the firm further past break-even without adding fixed cost.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Rent increases to $750/mo if property tax passes through
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs $2,670 + $100 increase = $2,770. At $95/member, $2,770 / $95 = 29.2 → 30 members minimum.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Minimal. Well within buffer at 55 members.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Worst case - rent to $850/mo (unlikely per Iverson)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still fine. Iverson said this was unlikely but wanted to stress test.
